frameworks.name: retrieval-induced forgetting
frameworks.one_line: reactivating a memory alters related, non-target memories from their original form

frameworks.name: constructive memory
frameworks.one_line: human memory is reconstructive rather than a passive readout of the past

core_question: What is the hidden cognitive cost of reminiscing — does the act of recall itself distort related memories?
key_assumptions: memory retrieval is an active reconstructive process, not passive readout
memories related to a recall target are co-reactivated and thus susceptible to change
key_findings: reactivating memories can change them away from the original event
memories not directly targeted but related to the recall target are also affected
errors of conflating related episodes are common in everyday remembering
interpretations: reminiscence carries a hidden mnemonic cost: recall reshapes related memories without an external manipulator
constructive nature of human memory makes retrieval-induced distortion a routine, not exotic, phenomenon
